_disclosure: SYNTHETIC DATA. All institution names are fictional. Routing numbers are constructed to be non-resolvable: prefixed '999' (an unallocated ABA prefix block) and deliberately fail the ABA routing-number checksum. Do not treat any value in this file as real. The per-institution 'cap' field is illustrative of how an individual RTP participant's send limit could sit below the network-wide ceiling; the $10,000,000 network ceiling itself is a verified, cited figure (see /docs/verified_constants.md).
network_cap_source: The Clearing House raised the RTP network per-transaction limit from $1,000,000 to $10,000,000, effective February 2025.

participants.notes: Standard active participant, no reduced cap.
Standard active participant, no reduced cap. Used as debtor institution in most sample scenarios.
INTENTIONALLY INACTIVE for test purposes: exited the RTP network. Drives the Reach-pillar 'destination not RTP-enabled' HOLD scenario.

INTENTIONALLY set below the $10M network ceiling to test institution-specific cap enforcement, distinct from the network-wide cap. A smaller regional participant electing a conservative send limit is realistic behavior per the network's design (individual institutions may adopt the higher ceiling gradually).
